mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 55% in declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which immediately pressures corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based domestic demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 65% of observations during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window despite consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high inflation threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm synchronized macroeconomic demand strength, which directly improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to yield a positive return frequency above 55% over the 20-day horizon in mid-cycle conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production index decline below 0.1 percent signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side contraction, which immediately reduces corporate output expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative supply signal.
